title: 10 best herbal creams for heart pain

remedy_properties: Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects: Ginkgo biloba's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce inflammation in blood vessels, which can contribute to heart pain by improving circulation and reducing oxidative stress.
Platelet aggregation inhibition: Ginkgo biloba has been shown to inhibit platelet aggregation, which can help prevent the formation of blood clots that can lead to heart attacks or strokes.

Cardioprotective compounds: The cream contains a variety of cardioprotective compounds, such as flavonoids and terpenes, which may help reduce cardiac arrhythmias, improve blood flow, and lower blood pressure.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba, also known as maidenhair tree, creams helps with heart pain because of its potent antioxidant properties. The cream's active ingredients, flavonoids and terpenoids, improve blood flow and reduce inflammation, which are key factors in alleviating heart pain. Ginkgo biloba creams also have anti-platelet and anti-clotting effects, which help to prevent platelets from sticking together and forming blood clots. This can help to reduce the severity and frequency of heart pain episodes, promoting overall cardiovascular health and well-being.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in blood vessels, contributing to the alleviation of heart pain.
Bilobalide: A sesquiterpene trimer that acts as a vasodilator, helping to widen blood vessels and improve blood flow to the heart, thus reducing the severity of heart pain.

Quercetin: A type of flavonoid that has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce the risk of blood clots and inflammation in blood vessels, contributing to the alleviation of heart pain.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are the most commonly used part due to their high flavonoid and terpenoid content, which are believed to contribute to their cardiovascular benefits.
Seeds: Ginkgo seeds are used to make creams as they contain ginkgolic acids, which have been traditionally used to treat circulatory issues and inflammation.
Barks: The barks of the Ginkgo biloba tree are also used in creams as they contain flavonoids and terpenoids that may help alleviate heart pain and improve cardiovascular health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 cups of fresh ginkgo biloba leaves from the plant at dawn when dew is still present on the leaves.
Dry the ginkgo biloba leaves in a low-temperature oven at 150f for 2 hours to preserve their properties.
Combine 1/4 cup of dried ginkgo biloba leaves with 1/2 cup of distilled water in a saucepan and simmer for 10 minutes.
Strain the liquid mixture through a cheesecloth and discard the solids to obtain a concentrated herbal extract.
Mix 2 tablespoons of the herbal extract with 2 tablespoons of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of coconut oil to create a smooth cream.

remedy_properties: Curcumin: Curcumin, a polyphenol compound found in turmeric, has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the cardiovascular system, alleviating heart pain.

Corticosteroid inhibition: Turmeric creams contain compounds that inhibit corticosteroids, which are natural hormones that promote inflammation. By inhibiting corticosteroids, turmeric creams can reduce inflammation and alleviate heart pain caused by conditions such as angina.

Cardioprotective flavonoids: Herbal turmeric creams often contain flavonoids like kaempferol and quercetin, which have been shown to protect against cardiovascular disease by improving blood vessel function, reducing blood pressure, and inhibiting platelet aggregation, all of which can contribute to reduced heart pain.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa, also known as turmeric, creams helps with heart pain because of its anti-inflammatory properties. The active compound curcumin in turmeric reduces inflammation and prevents the formation of blood clots, thereby alleviating heart pain. Curcumin also has antioxidant properties, which help to protect the heart by neutralizing free radicals that can damage heart cells. Additionally, turmeric has been found to improve blood flow and lower cholesterol levels, further contributing to its heart-pain-relieving properties. This natural remedy offers a promising alternative for heart pain management.
remedy_constituents: Curcumin: A polyphenolic compound with potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which helps reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the cardiovascular system, thereby alleviating heart pain.

Demethoxycurcumin (DMC): A curcuminoid with anti-inflammatory and anti-platelet properties, which helps prevent blood clots and reduce inflammation in blood vessels, leading to a decrease in heart pain.

Beta-Caryophyllene: A sesquiterpene with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which helps reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the cardiovascular system, thereby alleviating heart pain and improving overall cardiovascular health.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of Curcuma longa contain curcumin, a bioactive compound that has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making it effective in reducing heart pain.
Roots: The roots of Curcuma longa are also a source of curcumin, which has been shown to improve cardiovascular health by reducing inflammation and improving blood flow.
Leaves: The leaves of Curcuma longa contain other bioactive compounds, including gingerols and shogaols, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate heart pain.
remedy_recipe: Grind 2 tablespoons of dried curcuma longa tubers into a fine powder in a spice grinder for 30 seconds.
Mix the curcuma longa powder with 2 tablespoons of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture in the double boiler over low heat for 10 to 15 minutes or until the beeswax melts completely.
Remove the double boiler from the heat and let the mixture cool and thicken for 10 to 15 minutes.
Whip 2 tablespoons of aloe vera gel and 2 tablespoons of the cooled curcuma longa mixture together in a blender until smooth.

remedy_properties: Salicin: Salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, helps relieve heart pain by reducing inflammation and thinning the blood, which improves circulation and reduces pressure on the heart.

Antioxidants: The antioxidants present in white willow creams help protect the heart from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to conditions like atherosclerosis (hardening of the arteries) and cardiac arrhythmias.

Cardioactive compounds: White willow contains various cardioactive compounds that help dilate blood vessels, improve circulation, and reduce blood pressure, all of which can help alleviate heart pain.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Salix alba, also known as white willow, creams helps with heart pain because it contains salicin, a natural compound similar to aspirin. Salicin is absorbed into the bloodstream, where it helps to reduce inflammation and relieve pain. It targets the root cause of heart pain, whether it's due to inflammation, nerve damage, or other factors. By providing a natural and non-invasive solution, Salix alba creams can offer relief from heart pain, promoting a more comfortable and peaceful state.
remedy_constituents: Salicin: Salicin, a phenolic glycoside, helps with heart pain by reducing inflammation and pain through its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties.
Salicylic acid: Salicylic acid, a phenolic acid, helps with heart pain by inhibiting the production of prostaglandins, which are mediators of pain and inflammation, thereby providing pain relief.
Tannins: Tannins, a type of polyphenol, help with heart pain by reducing inflammation and preventing the formation of blood clots, which can contribute to heart pain and cardiovascular disorders.
remedy_parts: Barks: The barks are used to make creams for heart pain due to their anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: The leaves are used due to their ability to reduce inflammation and ease heart-related pain.
Buds: The buds are used to create creams for heart pain because of their purported ability to improve circulation and reduce inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 25g of dried salix alba bark and 10g of beeswax in the morning.
Infuse 50g of dried salix alba bark in 100ml of hot distilled water for 10 minutes.
Combine 20g of infused salix alba liquid with 20g of glycerin and 10g of vitamin e oil.
Mix the combined liquid with 40g of melted beeswax and 10g of candelilla wax.
Pour the mixture into a clean container and let it solidify at room temperature for 30 minutes.

remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: St. John's Wort cream has antioxidant properties that help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to heart pain by protecting the cardiovascular system from damage.

Vasodilatory effects: The active compounds in St. John's Wort cream, such as hypericin, dilate blood vessels, improving circulation and reducing pressure on the heart, which can alleviate symptoms of heart pain like angina.

Anti-inflammatory properties: St. John's Wort cream has anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce inflammation and swelling in the cardiovascular system, which can contribute to heart pain by decreasing blood flow and oxygen supply to the heart muscle.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hypericum perforatum, also known as St John's Wort, creams helps with heart pain because of its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The active compounds in the herb, such as hyperforin and hypericin, have been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain in the cardiovascular system. By blocking the production of pain-causing chemicals, these compounds provide relief from heart pain, making Hypericum perforatum creams a potential natural remedy for individuals suffering from cardiac conditions. Its benefits also help to soothe and calm the nervous system.
remedy_constituents: Hyperforin: This phenolic compound has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the heart, contributing to pain relief.
N-alkylamides: These compounds have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help block pain signals and reduce inflammation in the heart, providing relief from pain.
Quercetin: This flavonoid has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help protect the heart from oxidative damage and reduce inflammation, contributing to pain relief.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in hypericin and hyperforin, compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making them effective in soothing heart pain.
Flowers: Contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which contribute to their analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to reduce pain associated with heart conditions.
Stems: Rich in hypericin and other bioactive compounds, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help alleviate heart pain and promote overall cardiovascular health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-20 grams of fresh hypericum perforatum flowers and leaves at the peak of flowering.
Dry the harvested hypericum perforatum at 40 degrees celsius for 2-3 hours to preserve the properties.
Mix 10 grams of dried hypericum perforatum with 10 grams of beeswax and 10 grams of coconut oil.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler at 60 degrees celsius for 30 minutes to melt the ingredients.
Strain and pour the mixture into jars and allow to cool completely before application.

remedy_properties: Flavonoids: Hawthorn's flavonoids, such as quercetin and epicatechin, help to improve blood flow to the heart by relaxing blood vessels and reducing inflammation, which can alleviate angina (chest pain) caused by reduced blood flow.

Procyanidins: Procyanidins in hawthorn have been shown to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to protect against cardiovascular damage, reduce blood pressure, and improve overall heart function.

Cardiac glycosides: Hawthorn's cardiac glycosides, such as digoxin and digitoxin, may help to slow down the heart rate and increase the force of contractions, which can alleviate symptoms of heart failure and reduce chest pain.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Crataegus monogyna, also known as hawthorn, creams helps with heart pain because they contain flavonoids and oligomeric proanthocyanidins, which are believed to improve blood flow and reduce inflammation in the cardiovascular system. The active compounds in hawthorn creams have been shown to help relax blood vessels, lower blood pressure, and reduce oxidative stress, all of which can contribute to a reduction in heart pain. This natural remedy has been used in traditional medicine for centuries to support cardiovascular health.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: Flavonoids, particularly quercetin and epicatechin, help alleviate heart pain by improving cardiovascular health, reducing inflammation, and enhancing blood vessel function.

Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids, such as chlorogenic acid and caffeic acid, exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the heart, thereby alleviating pain.

Oligomeric proanthocyanidins (OPCs): OPCs, a type of flavonoid polymer, help protect the heart by reducing inflammation, improving blood lipid profiles, and enhancing the elasticity of blood vessels, ultimately alleviating heart pain.
remedy_parts: Barks: Used due to their high content of flavonoids, which may help to improve cardiovascular health.
Leaves: Utilized for their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may contribute to heart pain relief.
Fruits: Employed for their high concentration of flavonoids and other compounds that may help to protect the heart and alleviate pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh leaves and 50g of flowers from mature crataegus monogyna plants on a sunny morning.
Dry the plant material in a warm air circulation oven at 40°c for 2 hours.
Steep 20g of dried leaves and flowers in 500ml of cold-pressed jojoba oil for 2 weeks.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth and discard the solids, then filter the liquid through a 0.2μm membrane.
Mix the filtered oil with 10% beeswax and 1% vitamin e oil to create a smooth and stable cream base.

remedy_properties: Valerenic acids: These compounds have a calming effect on the nervous system, helping to reduce anxiety and stress, which can contribute to heart pain by increasing blood pressure and cardiac workload.
Isovaleric acid: This compound has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce inflammation in the blood vessels and heart tissue that may be contributing to heart pain.

Essential oils: The essential oils present in valerian cream, such as borneol and bornyl acetate, have a sedative effect on the cardiovascular system, helping to reduce palpitations, tachycardia, and hypertension, which can exacerbate heart pain.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valeriana officinalis, also known as valerian, creams helps with heart pain because of its unique ability to promote relaxation and reduce anxiety. The root extract of valerian contains valerenic acid, which interacts with the body's GABA receptors, calming the nervous system and reducing stress-induced heart palpitations. By promoting a sense of calm, valerian creams may help alleviate heart pain caused by anxiety and stress, providing relief for individuals experiencing heart-related discomfort. This natural remedy may also improve overall well-being and promote a restful night's sleep.
remedy_constituents: Valerenic acid: This valerenic acid helps with heart pain by inhibiting the activity of the GABA receptor antagonist, thereby reducing anxiety and stress, which are known contributors to heart pain.
Valeranone: Valeranone has sedative and anxiolytic properties, which can help alleviate heart pain by promoting relaxation and reducing muscle tension.

Valeranone's isovaleramide derivative: Isovaleramide, a derivative of valeranone, has been shown to have a positive effect on heart health by reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular function.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Valeriana officinalis are rich in valerenic acids, which are believed to have sedative and anti-anxiety effects that can help alleviate heart pain.
Barks: The barks of Valeriana officinalis contain valerian compounds that have been used in traditional medicine to treat anxiety and stress-related heart pain.

Leaves: Valeriana officinalis leaves are also used in creams for heart pain due to their content of valerenic acids and flavonoids, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of valeriana officinalis roots in the spring or fall when the plant is dormant.
Dry the harvested roots in a cool dark place for 2 to 3 weeks to remove moisture.
Combine the dried roots with 100ml of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture under low heat for 2 to 3 hours to infuse the oil with the plant's properties.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth and add 5g of beeswax to create a thick creamy texture.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Ginger creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds like gingerol and shogaol, which help reduce inflammation in the body, which can contribute to heart pain (angina) by reducing blood flow to the heart.

Antioxidant properties: Herbal ginger creams have antioxidant properties that help protect the heart from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to cardiovascular disease and heart pain.

Cardiovascular-protective compounds: Ginger contains compounds like 6-gingerol and 8-gingerol, which have been shown to have a positive effect on blood flow and may help reduce the risk of heart disease and related symptoms such as heart pain.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale, also known as ginger, creams helps with heart pain because of its anti-inflammatory properties. The active compounds in ginger, such as gingerol and shogaol, have been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain in the cardiovascular system. The cream's topical application allows for direct delivery of these compounds to the affected area, providing fast and effective relief from heart pain caused by conditions like angina or myocardial infarction. This natural remedy can be a valuable alternative to traditional pain management methods.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: Gingerols, specifically 6-gingerol and 8-gingerol, have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce pain and inflammation associated with heart conditions.
Shogaols: Shogaols, particularly 6-shogaol, have potent anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects that help alleviate heart pain by reducing inflammation and blocking pain signals.
Zingerone: Zingerone, a compound derived from ginger, has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help protect against heart disease by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation.
remedy_parts: Buds: Ginger buds are used in traditional medicine to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain, particularly in the heart and cardiovascular system, due to their high antioxidant and anti-inflammatory content.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 fresh zingiber officinale rhizomes and wash them thoroughly under cold running water for 2 minutes.
Slice the rhizomes into thin pieces using a sharp knife and then dry them in a low-temperature oven at 150°f for 2 hours.
Combine the dried zingiber officinale pieces with 2 tablespoons of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of coconut oil in a double boiler over low heat for 10 minutes.
Stir the mixture continuously until the beeswax and coconut oil are fully incorporated and melted.
Pour the mixture into small tin containers and let it cool and solidify at room temperature for 30 minutes.

remedy_properties: Adaptogenic Properties: Ashwagandha cream's adaptogenic properties help to reduce stress and anxiety, which can contribute to heart pain by lowering cortisol levels and promoting relaxation.
Antioxidant Properties: Ashwagandha cream's antioxidant properties help to reduce inflammation in the blood vessels, which can cause heart pain by improving blood flow and reducing oxidative stress.

Cardio-protective Properties: Ashwagandha cream's cardio-protective properties help to improve cardiovascular function by lowering cholesterol levels, reducing blood pressure, and preventing cardiac fibrosis, all of which can contribute to heart pain.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Withania somnifera, also known as ashwagandha, creams helps with heart pain because of its adaptogenic properties and anti-inflammatory compounds. The herb contains withanolides, which have been shown to improve blood circulation, reduce oxidative stress, and mitigate inflammation in the cardiovascular system. By promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety, ashwagandha creams can also help alleviate angina symptoms, such as chest tightness and shortness of breath. Regular use may also help lower blood pressure and improve overall cardiovascular health.
remedy_constituents: Withanolides: Withanolides, a class of steroidal lactones, have been shown to have cardioprotective effects by reducing oxidative stress, inflammation, and platelet aggregation, thus helping to alleviate heart pain.

Withaferin A: Withaferin A, an alkaloid compound, exhibits anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the cardiovascular system, thereby providing relief from heart pain.

Saponins: Saponins, a type of glycoside, have been found to have a positive effect on cardiovascular health by reducing triglyceride levels, improving lipid profiles, and preventing the formation of atherosclerotic plaques, all of which can contribute to heart pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Withania somnifera are used to make creams for heart pain due to their high content of withanolides, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
Leaves: The leaves are used due to their high content of withanolides and other bioactive compounds, which help to reduce inflammation and alleviate heart pain.
Seeds: The seeds are used due to their high content of withanolides and other bioactive compounds, which help to reduce inflammation and alleviate heart pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500 grams of dried withania somnifera root and 50 grams of beeswax in the early morning.
Grind the withania somnifera root into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 10 minutes.
Combine the ground powder with 50 grams of shea butter and 20 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Stir the mixture continuously over low heat for 30 minutes to create a smooth consistency.
Remove the mixture from heat and add 10 drops of vitamin e oil and 20 grams of jojoba oil before pouring it into a tin container.

remedy_properties: Lavender's analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties: English lavender contains compounds like linalool and linalyl acetate, which have been shown to reduce pain and inflammation in the body, potentially alleviating heart pain symptoms.

Antioxidant activity: Lavender's antioxidant properties help protect the body from free radicals that can cause oxidative stress, which is thought to contribute to various cardiovascular diseases and heart pain conditions.

Cardiovascular relaxation and calming effects: English lavender's relaxing and calming effects on the nervous system may help reduce stress and anxiety, which are common triggers for heart pain episodes, while also promoting a sense of calm and relaxation.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia, also known as English lavender, creams helps with heart pain because of its calming and anti-inflammatory properties. The active compounds in Lavandula angustifolia, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, have been shown to reduce muscle spasms and relax the cardiovascular system. This can help alleviate the pain associated with heart conditions, such as angina and palpitations. Additionally, the calming effect of Lavandula angustifolia can help reduce stress and anxiety, which can contribute to heart pain.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: A terpene that acts as a vasodilator, helping to relax blood vessels and reduce inflammation, which can alleviate heart pain caused by cardiovascular issues.
Linalyl acetate: A terpene that has a calming effect on the nervous system, reducing stress and anxiety, which are often associated with heart pain and cardiovascular conditions.

Lavandulol: A monoterpenol that has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the cardiovascular system, potentially alleviating heart pain.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain volatile oils, which have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, helping to alleviate heart pain.
Flowers: Rich in linalool and linalyl acetate, the flowers have a calming effect on the nervous system, reducing heart pain and stress.
Seeds: Containing essential oils and antioxidants, the seeds help reduce inflammation and promote relaxation, which can ease heart pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 liter of fresh lavandula angustifolia flowers at dawn when the dew is still present for 2 hours.
Dry the fresh flowers in a single layer at 45 degrees celsius for 2 hours in a dehydrator.
Steep 1 cup of dried flowers in 1 liter of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil at 60 degrees celsius for 2 hours.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth and discard the solids, then transfer the oil to a clean container.
Blend 250 grams of beeswax, 200 grams of shea butter, and the infused oil in a double boiler at 60 degrees celsius for 30 minutes.

remedy_properties: Menthol: Menthol helps to relieve chest tightness and pain by dilating blood vessels, improving blood flow to the heart and reducing inflammation in the coronary arteries.

Eucalyptol: Eucalyptol has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation in the cardiovascular system, which can contribute to heart pain, while also opening up airways to improve breathing and oxygenation of the body.
Menthone: Menthone helps to relax the smooth muscle in the coronary arteries, reducing spasms and improving blood flow to the heart, which can help alleviate angina-like symptoms.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita, also known as peppermint, creams helps with heart pain because it contains menthol, which has natural anti-inflammatory properties that can help to relax the muscles and improve blood flow to the heart. The cooling sensation of peppermint can also help to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain. Additionally, the aroma of peppermint can help to reduce stress and anxiety, which are common causes of heart pain. By combining these properties, peppermint creams can provide relief from heart pain and promote overall cardiovascular health.
remedy_constituents: Menthol: Menthol helps to relax muscles and reduce pain by blocking the production of pain-causing chemicals in the body, providing quick relief from heart pain.

Rosmarinic acid (a phenolic compound): Rosmarinic acid has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the body, thereby alleviating heart pain and promoting overall cardiovascular health.

Caryophyllene oxide (a sesquiterpene): Caryophyllene oxide has anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties, which help to reduce pain and inflammation in the heart and surrounding tissues, providing long-term relief from heart pain.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are used due to their high content of menthol, which has analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties.
Stems: Stems are used as they contain menthone, which helps to relax muscles and reduce pain.
Roots: Roots are used due to their high concentration of menthol and menthone, which together provide pain relief and anti-inflammatory benefits.
remedy_recipe: Harvest a sufficient quantity of fresh peppermint leaves approximately 1 pound by mid-morning.
Distill the fresh peppermint leaves in a steam distillation apparatus for 3 hours.
Combine the peppermint essential oil with a carrier oil in a 1:9 ratio by volume for 30 minutes.
Add a preservative such as phenonip to the peppermint oil mixture in a 1% concentration by weight for 15 minutes.
Pour the mixture into a heat-resistant container and place it in a refrigerator to chill and set for 2 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile creams helps with heart pain because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which soothe the nervous system and reduce stress and anxiety. The cream's apigenin and luteolin compounds bind to receptors in the brain, promoting relaxation and calming the mind and body. By reducing tension and promoting a sense of calm, chamomile creams can help alleviate heart pain associated with angina, mitral valve prolapse, and other cardiovascular conditions, ultimately providing relief from discomfort and improving overall well-being.
remedy_properties: Anti-Inflammatory Properties: Chamomile creams reduce inflammation, which can alleviate chest pain and discomfort associated with conditions like angina or pericarditis.
Antioxidant Properties: Chamomile's antioxidant compounds help protect the heart from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to cardiovascular disease and related heart pain.

Anxiolytic (Stress-Relieving) Properties: Chamomile creams have a calming effect on the nervous system, helping to reduce stress and anxiety, which are common triggers for heart pain in individuals with anxiety disorders or hypertension.
remedy_parts: Roots: Chamomile roots have been traditionally used in herbal remedies to calm anxiety and promote relaxation, making them effective for soothing heart pain.
Flowers: The flowers of the chamomile plant contain apigenin, an anti-inflammatory compound that helps reduce swelling and alleviate heart pain.
remedy_recipe: Weigh out one cup of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers and grind them into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered chamomile with two tablespoons of jojoba oil in a small bowl until well combined.
Add three tablespoons of beeswax pellets to the mixture and stir over low heat for 10-15 minutes or until melted.
Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool until it reaches a temperature of 120°f to 140°f before whipping.
Whip the mixture using an electric mixer on high speed for 2-3 minutes until it thickens into a smooth cream-like consistency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow creams helps with heart pain because of its unique composition. The herbal extract, derived from the root of the marshmallow plant, contains mucilage, a thick, soothing substance that coats and calms the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and discomfort. This calming effect can also translate to the cardiovascular system, where it may help alleviate heartburn, acid reflux, and even angina-like chest pain by neutralizing acidity and relaxing blood vessels. As a natural remedy, marshmallow creams offer a gentle yet effective way to soothe heart-related distress.
remedy_properties: Althaea officinalis extract: Rich in mucilage, which forms a protective barrier on the digestive tract lining, soothing inflammation and irritation, thereby reducing chest discomfort and pain associated with heartburn and acid reflux.

Glycyrrhizin: A key compound found in licorice root, known for its anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the cardiovascular system, contributing to reduced heart pain and improved overall heart health.

Aloe vera gel: Contains compounds like aloin and aloe-emodin, which have been shown to improve blood lipid profiles, reducing triglycerides and LDL cholesterol levels, thereby helping to prevent the formation of plaque in arteries and alleviate heart pain caused by coronary artery disease.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in mucilage, a thick, protective substance that helps soothe and calm irritated tissues, making it effective in reducing heart pain.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes contain flavonoids and saponins, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate the discomfort associated with heart pain.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried marshmallow root althaea officinalis 10 grams and carrier oil sweet almond oil 100 milliliters.
Combine the dried herb and carrier oil in a heatproof glass container and steep for 4 hours at room temperature.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another container to remove the solids and discard.
Add beeswax pellets 20 grams and vitamin e oil 5 milliliters to the liquid extract and melt in a double boiler.
Pour the hot mixture into sterilized glass jars fill to the top and allow to cool and solidify for at least 30 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maypop creams helps with heart pain because they contain potent natural compounds that work to relax the blood vessels, reducing inflammation and promoting a healthy circulatory system. The cream's active ingredients, including flavonoids and terpenes, help to dilate the arteries, improving blood flow and reducing pressure on the heart. This natural relaxation of the blood vessels can provide significant relief from chronic pain and discomfort associated with cardiovascular conditions.
remedy_properties: Passionflower: The passionflower extract in Maypop cream has a sedative effect, which can help to reduce anxiety and stress-related chest pain associated with heart conditions.
Cramp bark: Cramp bark is known for its ability to relax muscle spasms and ease cramping, making it effective in relieving angina pain caused by coronary artery disease.
Capsaicin: Capsaicin, a compound found in Maypop cream, has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce inflammation and swelling in blood vessels, which can contribute to heart pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, Maypop roots have been traditionally used to alleviate symptoms of cardiovascular disease, including heart pain.
Barks: Containing flavonoids and terpenes, Maypop barks may help reduce inflammation and improve blood flow, providing relief from heart pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry 30 grams of fresh passiflora incarnata leaves and flowers under shade at room temperature for three days.
Combine the dried botanicals with 10 grams of sweet almond oil in a clean glass jar and store in the refrigerator for four hours.
Decant the mixture into a heat-resistant bowl and gently warm it over low heat for 10 minutes, then let it cool to 70°f.
Stir 2 tablespoons of beeswax pellets and 1 tablespoon of coconut oil into the cooled passiflora mixture, ensuring complete emulsification within five minutes.
Transfer the herbal cream to sterilized glass jars and allow it to cool and set at room temperature for two hours before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Saffron creams helps with heart pain because of its unique ability to reduce inflammation and improve blood flow. The natural anti-inflammatory properties of saffron help to calm inflamed blood vessels, reducing pressure on the heart and alleviating symptoms of angina. Additionally, the cream's vasodilating effects allow for increased oxygen delivery to the heart muscle, reducing strain and discomfort. By promoting healthy circulation and reducing inflammation, herbal saffron creams provide natural relief from heart pain, offering an alternative solution to traditional pharmaceutical treatments.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Saffron's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as crocin and crocetin, help reduce inflammation in the body, which can contribute to heart pain by alleviating swelling and discomfort.
Antioxidant properties: Saffron's antioxidants combat free radicals that damage blood vessels and tissues, reducing oxidative stress that can lead to cardiovascular disease and related heart pain.

Cardiovascular protective properties: Saffron's bioactive compounds have been shown to improve blood flow, lower blood pressure, and prevent platelet aggregation, all of which help reduce the risk of heart attacks, strokes, and other cardiovascular events that may cause heart pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the saffron plant are used due to their ability to reduce inflammation and improve blood flow, making them effective in alleviating heart pain.

Barks: The barks of the saffron plant contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help to relax blood vessels and reduce cardiac stress, making them a valuable addition to medicinal creams for heart pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh saffron flowers of crocus sativus and dry them in a warm oven at 150°f for 24 hours.
Grind dried saffron into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle, yielding approximately 10 grams per ounce.
Combine ground saffron with coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler, heating the mixture to 180°f for 30 minutes.
Remove from heat and let cool slightly before whipping the mixture until it thickens and emulsifies, taking approximately 15 minutes.
Pour the cream into sterilized glass jars, seal tightly, and store in the refrigerator at 40°f or lower for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tree turmeric creams helps with heart pain because they contain curcumin, a powerful anti-inflammatory compound found in the herb turmeric. Curcumin has been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of cardiovascular disease, including chest pain and angina. By reducing inflammation and improving blood flow, tree turmeric creams can help to relieve constriction in the coronary arteries, reducing the risk of heart attacks and strokes. Additionally, curcumin's antioxidant properties help to protect against oxidative stress and damage to the cardiovascular system.
remedy_properties: Curcumin: A powerful anti-inflammatory compound that reduces inflammation in the blood vessels, which can help alleviate heart pain caused by conditions like angina and coronary artery disease.

Vasodilatory effects: Turmeric's active compounds, such as curcumin, cause blood vessels to dilate (widen), improving blood flow and reducing the risk of cardiovascular events that can cause chest pain.
Antioxidant properties: Turmeric's antioxidant content helps protect against oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals in the body, which can contribute to heart disease and related pain.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Turmeric rhyzomes are rich in curcuminoids, which have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help alleviate heart pain by reducing inflammation and improving blood flow.
Roots: Turmeric roots contain a variety of compounds that have been shown to have cardio-protective effects, including the ability to lower triglycerides and reduce platelet aggregation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried berberis aristata roots weighing about 250 grams and chop them into small pieces.
Boil the chopped roots in 1000 milliliters of distilled water for 30 minutes to create a decoction.
Strain the decoction through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another container leaving behind 500 milliliters of liquid.
Mix 250 grams of beeswax with 125 grams of coconut oil and heat them in a double boiler until melted combining with the berberis aristata infusion.
Allow the mixture to cool and solidify at room temperature for two hours before whipping into a smooth creamy consistency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ceylon cinnamon creams helps with heart pain because of its unique blend of active compounds, particularly cinnamaldehyde. This compound has been shown to have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of heart pain by reducing inflammation in the blood vessels and improving blood flow. Additionally, Ceylon cinnamon has been found to improve circulation, lower triglycerides, and increase good cholesterol levels, all of which can contribute to a healthier heart and reduced risk of heart disease.
remedy_properties: Cinnamaldehyde: This compound has been shown to reduce inflammation and platelet aggregation, which can contribute to heart pain by improving blood flow and reducing the risk of blood clots.

Magnesium content: Cinnamon is a rich source of magnesium, which helps to relax blood vessels and improve blood flow, reducing the occurrence of angina attacks and improving overall cardiovascular health.

Antioxidant activity: The antioxidants present in cinnamon have been shown to protect against oxidative stress and damage to the heart muscle cells, reducing inflammation and improving cardiac function.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rich in cinnamaldehyde, a compound that has been shown to reduce inflammation and improve blood flow, making it effective in alleviating heart pain.
Barks: Containing compounds like cinnamic acid and coumarin, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, helping to soothe and protect the cardiovascular system.
remedy_recipe: Measure out two teaspoons of dried cinnamomum verum bark and grind into fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine the ground cinnamomum verum with 4 ounces of cold-pressed coconut oil in a double boiler or a heat-resistant glass bowl set over simmering water.
Stir the mixture constantly for 30 minutes to allow the herbal extract to infuse into the coconut oil.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container, squeezing the cheesecloth to extract as much of the herb-infused oil as possible.
Allow the cream to cool and thicken at room temperature for 60 minutes before transferring it to an airtight container and storing in the refrigerator for up to two weeks.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel creams helps with heart pain because it contains anethole, a compound that relaxes the smooth muscle in blood vessels, improving circulation and reducing inflammation. The antispasmodic properties of fennel also help to alleviate chest tightness and discomfort associated with angina and heart palpitations. Additionally, fennel's natural anti-inflammatory effects may help reduce swelling and pain in the cardiovascular system, providing relief from chronic heart conditions such as atherosclerosis and hypertension.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Fennel cream's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce inflammation in the cardiovascular system, which can contribute to heart pain by reducing the swelling and discomfort associated with conditions like angina.

Antioxidant activity: The antioxidant properties of fennel cream neutralize free radicals that can damage blood vessels and increase the risk of heart disease, thereby helping to alleviate heart pain caused by oxidative stress.

Cardiac glycosides: Fennel contains cardiac glycosides like digoxin, which have been shown to help regulate heart rhythm and reduce symptoms of heart failure, such as shortness of breath, fatigue, and chest pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: Fennel roots are rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help reduce inflammation and improve blood circulation, making them effective in relieving heart pain.
Barks: Fennel barks contain sesquiterpenes, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate chest pain and discomfort associated with heart conditions.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried foeniculum vulgare seeds in sufficient quantity for the desired cream amount and store them in an airtight container.
Combine one part dried foeniculum vulgare seeds with two parts carrier oil such as sweet almond or coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes to allow the seeds to infuse their active compounds into the oil, stirring occasionally.
Strain the oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container, discarding the solids and reserving the infused oil for use in the cream.
Mix one part of the infused oil with three parts of beeswax, heating gently until melted, then pour into tins or jars to cool and harden completely.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice creams helps with heart pain because they contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory properties. This compound helps to reduce inflammation in the blood vessels, which can contribute to angina and other forms of cardiovascular disease. Additionally, licorice creams also contain flavonoids, which have antioxidant effects that help to protect the heart from damage caused by free radicals. By reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, herbal licorice creams may help to alleviate heart pain and promote overall cardiovascular health.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: This compound has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the blood vessels and alleviate symptoms of angina and heart failure.

Flavonoids: Herbal licorice creams contain flavonoids, which are antioxidants that can help protect against oxidative stress and damage to the cardiovascular system, reducing the risk of heart disease and improving overall cardiac health.
Isomaltose: This compound has been shown to have anti-arrhythmic properties, meaning it can help regulate abnormal heart rhythms and reduce the severity of angina attacks.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain a compound called glycyrrhizin, which has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help alleviate heart pain by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress.
Barks: Licorice bark is rich in flavonoids and saponins, which have been shown to have vasodilatory effects, helping to improve blood flow and reduce the risk of heart disease.
remedy_recipe: Sterilize all equipment and workspace before preparing glycyrrhiza glabra cream for heart pain relief.
Combine 20% glycerin with 80% distilled water to create a hydrous phase and stir until fully dissolved.
Infuse 10 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra root powder in 100 milliliters of boiling ethanol for 30 minutes.
Filter the infused mixture through a coffee filter and combine it with the hydrous phase, then emulsify using lecithin at a ratio of 1:3.
Pour the final cream into sterilized containers and allow it to cool at room temperature for two hours before refrigerating for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary creams helps with heart pain because it contains compounds that improve blood flow to the heart, reducing inflammation and oxidative stress. The antioxidant properties of rosemary also help protect against damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate heart conditions. Additionally, rosemary's ability to relax blood vessels and reduce blood pressure further alleviates cardiac strain, providing relief from angina and other symptoms associated with heart pain. By promoting cardiovascular health, these creams offer a natural remedy for managing heart-related discomfort.
remedy_properties: Carnosic acid: A powerful antioxidant that helps to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the body, which can contribute to heart pain (angina) by improving blood flow and reducing damage to cardiac tissues.

Rosmarinic acid: A bioactive compound that has anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and vasodilatory effects, which can help to relieve chest pain by relaxing blood vessels, reducing inflammation, and improving cardiovascular function.

Camphor: A natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory agent that helps to reduce pain, discomfort, and swelling associated with heart conditions, such as angina or pericarditis, by blocking the production of prostaglandins (inflammatory compounds).
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing inflammation and improving circulation, which can help alleviate heart pain.
Barks: Containing volatile oils and flavonoids, they have been traditionally used to treat cardiovascular issues such as hypertension and cardiac arrhythmias, which can cause chest pain.
remedy_recipe: Gather 20 grams of dried rosmarinus officinalis leaves and 100ml of sweet almond oil in a clean glass jar.
Heat the sweet almond oil in a double boiler at 40°c for 10 minutes to decolonize it from any impurities.
Add the 20 grams of dried rosmarinus officinalis leaves to the warm sweet almond oil and steep for 30 minutes to infuse their medicinal properties.
Strain the infused mixture through cheesecloth into a clean glass container, pressing on the solids to extract as much oil as possible, and discard the solids after 5 minutes of straining.
Mix the strained herbal infusion with 10 grams of beeswax and 20ml of coconut oil in a small saucepan over low heat, stirring until melted and combined for 10 minutes to create the final cream.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for heart pain are topical remedies that help alleviate cardiac discomfort by reducing inflammation, improving circulation, and relaxing the muscles. The best herbal creams for heart pain include Capsicum annuum, Zingiber officinale, Ginkgo biloba, Aloe barbadensis, Valeriana officinalis, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Passiflora incarnata, Panax ginseng, Piper nigrum, and Ruscus aculeatus. If left untreated, heart pain can significantly impact daily life, causing anxiety, fatigue, and decreased productivity.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to heart pain that are treated with herbal creams are fibromyalgia, arthritis, and musculoskeletal disorders. Herbal creams containing ingredients like arnica, capsaicin, and menthol help alleviate symptoms such as joint stiffness, muscle spasms, and chronic pain. These topical treatments provide relief by reducing inflammation, promoting relaxation, and warming or cooling the affected area to ease discomfort.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal creams that help with heart pain is a blend of Arnica, St. John's Wort, and Frankincense. Arnica helps reduce inflammation and soothe the chest area, while St. John's Wort promotes emotional healing and reduces anxiety. Frankincense, with its anti-inflammatory properties, aids in pain relief and relaxation. Together, these herbal creams create a synergistic effect, addressing both physical and emotional aspects of heart pain. This combination can be applied topically to the affected area, providing relief and promoting overall well-being.

remedies.remedy_desc: Capsicum annuum creams helps with heart pain because they contain capsaicin, a natural compound that blocks the production of a chemical called substance P. This chemical transmits pain signals to the brain, making capsaicin a potent pain reliever. By reducing the flow of pain signals, capsaicin creams can provide quick relief from heart pain caused by conditions such as angina or myocardial infarction. This natural remedy can also promote blood flow and reduce inflammation, further alleviating heart-related discomfort.

Zingiber officinale creams helps with heart pain because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and relax tense muscles. The active compounds in ginger, such as gingerol and shogaol, have been shown to improve circulation and reduce pain by blocking the production of pain-causing chemicals. Additionally, ginger's antioxidant properties help protect the heart from damage and promote overall cardiovascular health. By reducing inflammation and improving circulation, ginger creams can provide relief from heart pain and promote a sense of well-being.

Ginkgo biloba creams helps with heart pain because they improve blood circulation by opening up blood vessels, allowing more oxygen and nutrients to reach the heart. This increased blood flow reduces inflammation and relaxes the heart muscles, providing relief from pain and discomfort. Additionally, Ginkgo biloba's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce oxidative stress, which can contribute to heart pain. Overall, Ginkgo biloba creams can provide quick and effective relief from heart pain, promoting overall cardiovascular well-being.

Aloe barbadensis creams helps with heart pain because they have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that reduce swelling and promote healing in the affected area. The cream's cooling effect can also help to relieve tension and soothe the muscles, providing quick relief from pain and discomfort. Additionally, Aloe vera contains compounds that improve circulation and reduce inflammation, which can help to alleviate heart pain caused by conditions such as angina or myocardial infarction. This natural remedy offers a potential alternative to conventional treatments.

Valeriana officinalis creams helps with heart pain because they contain valerenic acid and isovaleric acid, which have a calming effect on the nervous system. This reduces stress and anxiety that can contribute to heart pain. The anti-inflammatory properties in Valeriana officinalis creams also help to soothe and relax the muscles, reducing the discomfort associated with heart pain. Additionally, the creams may help to lower blood pressure and improve circulation, further alleviating heart pain and promoting overall cardiovascular health.

Glycyrrhiza glabra creams helps with heart pain because they contain licorice root extract, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and discomfort in the cardiovascular system. The flavonoids and glycosides present in the extract may also help to relax blood vessels, improve circulation, and lower blood pressure, all of which can contribute to a reduction in heart pain. Additionally, the cream's anti-oxidant properties may help to protect the heart from oxidative stress and damage.

Passiflora incarnata creams helps with heart pain because they contain the herb Passiflora incarnata, also known as purple passionflower. This herb has been used for centuries in traditional medicine to calm the nervous system and reduce anxiety, which can often contribute to heart pain. The creams work by relaxing the mind and body, reducing stress and inflammation, and promoting a sense of calm and well-being. By reducing anxiety and promoting relaxation, Passiflora incarnata creams can help alleviate heart pain and improve overall cardiovascular health.

Panax ginseng creams helps with heart pain because of its unique ability to promote blood circulation and reduce inflammation in the cardiovascular system. The active compounds in Panax ginseng, such as ginsenosides, have been shown to improve blood flow to the heart, reducing the severity of angina and other heart-related pain. Additionally, the cream's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce oxidative stress and promote healing in the cardiovascular tissue, making it a potential natural remedy for alleviating heart pain and promoting overall cardiovascular health.

Piper nigrum creams helps with heart pain because they contain bioactive compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The extracts from black pepper, a key ingredient in these creams, help reduce inflammation and improve blood flow to the heart, thereby alleviating pain. Additionally, the creams may also help to relax the muscles and improve cardiovascular health, further reducing the severity of heart pain. This natural approach can provide relief without the need for harsh pharmaceuticals.

Ruscus aculeatus creams helps with heart pain because they contain anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that soothe and calm the cardiovascular system. The herb's flavonoids and phenolic acids work together to improve blood flow, reduce inflammation, and relax the heart muscles, thus alleviating pain and discomfort associated with heart conditions. This natural remedy is believed to promote overall cardiovascular health, reducing the risk of heart disease and supporting a healthy heart function.
